name: mlb-faab-sizer Quality Rubric
description: Eight-criterion rubric evaluating FAAB bid sizing outputs. Each criterion scored 1 (poor), 3 (acceptable), 5 (excellent). Minimum acceptable overall average: 3.5. Minimum per-criterion floor: 3.0 for criteria 1, 2, and 5 (these are safety-critical).

criteria.name: Input Completeness
criteria.1: One or more required inputs missing or assumed without citation. FAAB remaining not verified against team-profile.md. Week number inferred rather than computed from today's date. Role_certainty or positional_need_fit used without source signal.
criteria.3: All required inputs present (acquisition_value, positional_need_fit, role_certainty, FAAB remaining, week number, situation label). Each value has a source but some are stale or lack timestamp.
criteria.5: All required inputs present, each tied to a named upstream signal (mlb-player-analyzer for acquisition_value and role_certainty; mlb-waiver-analyst for positional_need_fit) with computed_at timestamps. FAAB remaining reconciled against the latest team-profile.md entry. Week number computed from today's date. Regression index included when available.

criteria.name: Multiplier Justification
criteria.1: Urgency or season-pace multipliers used without rationale, or values outside the allowed ranges (urgency: 0.7-1.4, pace: 0.6-1.4). Default values used when situation clearly warrants an adjustment.
criteria.3: Both multipliers selected from the allowed ranges with brief rationale. Situation label maps reasonably to urgency_multiplier. Calendar bucket maps reasonably to season_pace_multiplier.
criteria.5: Both multipliers justified with explicit reference to the situation-label table (urgency) and calendar-bucket table (pace). Edge cases (late-April interpolation, eliminated-team September) handled explicitly. Contention status documented when relevant.

criteria.name: League Inflation Calibration
criteria.1: faab-log not read, or read but calibration not applied despite sufficient data. Or calibration applied without computing or disclosing league_inflation_ratio. Or calibration applied with <5 valid rows (over-calibration on thin data).
criteria.3: faab-log read, valid rows filtered, league_inflation_ratio computed and applied. Calibration confidence (low/medium/high) reported. Clamping to [0.6, 1.4] applied if triggered.
criteria.5: faab-log read; valid rows filtered with both winning_bid and industry_consensus_at_time; league_inflation_ratio computed as mean of ratios; calibration confidence reported based on row count; recency weighting applied if 10+ rows; calibration_clamp_triggered flagged if clamping fired; if <5 rows, explicitly skipped with low_calibration_data flag.

criteria.name: Formula Correctness
criteria.1: max_bid_fraction computed incorrectly -- wrong operations, missing factor, or unit error (e.g., using acquisition_value as-is rather than / 100). faab_rec_bid not derived from faab_max_bid x 0.6 + 1.
criteria.3: Core formulas implemented correctly. All five factors present. Rounding applied. Rec-bid uses 0.6 factor and +1 tie-breaker.
criteria.5: Core formulas implemented exactly per framework. Full formula trace shown in output (all factors, intermediate fractions, pre-round and post-round dollar values). Rec-bid +1 tie-breaker documented as Yahoo-specific. $0-bid edge case handled (if max = 0, rec = 0).

criteria.name: Guardrail Enforcement
criteria.1: April 40% cap not enforced before July. Speculation 20% cap not enforced on low role_certainty targets. Variant divergence ignored. $0 bids not considered even when appropriate.
criteria.3: April 40%, speculation 20%, variant divergence, regression luck, and role-certainty floor all checked. Any triggered guardrail is flagged in the output. Bid values modified accordingly.
criteria.5: All 8 guardrails implemented (April 40%, speculation 20%, variant divergence, $0 default, budget floor, regression luck, role-certainty floor, FAAB remaining cap). Each triggered guardrail is named, original and capped values shown, and reflected in the user-facing rationale. No silent violations.

criteria.name: Output Structure
criteria.1: Bid values provided without formula trace, multipliers, or confidence. No signal file written. User-facing rationale missing or in jargon.
criteria.3: Output includes faab_rec_bid, faab_max_bid, the multipliers used, and any guardrail flags. User-facing rationale provided in plain English.
criteria.5: Full output block per template.md including formula_trace (all intermediate values), multipliers (both base and calibrated), guardrails_triggered array, calibration block, confidence score, and source_urls. Signal file written via mlb-signal-emitter. User brief follows the beginner-safe template, ending in BID $X verb with a ceiling and a fallback.

criteria.name: Beginner Communication
criteria.1: Uses baseball jargon (xwOBA, FIP, BABIP, platoon splits) without translation. Output reads like a scouting report, not a decision. No verb at the end.
criteria.3: User-facing rationale is mostly plain English. Ends in BID $X. Some jargon translated inline.
criteria.5: Every baseball or fantasy-specific term translated inline on first use. Rationale is 2-4 sentences, beginner-safe, answers 'what, why, ceiling, fallback'. Ends in explicit BID $X with ceiling $Y. Source URLs cited.

criteria.name: Logging and Traceability
criteria.1: Bid decision not logged. faab-log not updated after bid placement. No record of which signals or faab-log rows were used.
criteria.3: Decision logged to tracker/decisions-log.md with inputs and outputs. faab-log append-entry prepared for post-bid update.
criteria.5: Decision logged via mlb-decision-logger with full inputs, multipliers, guardrail flags, variants, synthesis, red-team findings, confidence, and will_verify_on date. faab-log append-entry generated and ready for post-bid append. Signal file linked. Reproducible: another agent could re-run the computation from the logged data and get the same bid.

scoring.weights_note: Formula Correctness and Guardrail Enforcement are weighted 1.5x because they are the load-bearing computations -- an error here directly misstates the bid. All other criteria weighted 1.0x.

common_failure_modes.symptom: faab-log has 8+ valid entries but season_pace_multiplier uses the base value unchanged
common_failure_modes.detection: calibration block in output shows faab_log_entries_read >= 5 but season_pace_multiplier_calibrated == season_pace_multiplier_base
common_failure_modes.fix: Always apply calibration when >= 5 valid rows exist. Clamp to [0.6, 1.4] after applying.

common_failure_modes.symptom: faab_max_bid exceeds 40% of FAAB remaining and week <= 13 without guardrail flag
common_failure_modes.detection: Check faab_max_bid / FAAB_remaining and week_number; if ratio > 0.40 and week <= 13, the april_40pct_cap_triggered flag must be present
common_failure_modes.fix: Enforce guardrail 1. Cap max_bid at 40% and recompute rec_bid from the capped max. Flag in output.

common_failure_modes.symptom: role_certainty < 30 and faab_max_bid > 20% of FAAB remaining, no flag
common_failure_modes.detection: Inspect role_certainty and faab_max_bid / FAAB_remaining; speculation_20pct_cap_triggered must fire when both conditions met
common_failure_modes.fix: Enforce guardrail 2. Cap max at 20% and flag.

common_failure_modes.symptom: Output shows only final bid amounts with no intermediate values
common_failure_modes.detection: formula_trace field missing or partial in output block
common_failure_modes.fix: Include every intermediate fraction, the multipliers (base and calibrated), and pre-round dollar amount. Reproducibility requires the trace.

common_failure_modes.symptom: User brief uses terms like 'xwOBA regression', 'BABIP lucky', 'platoon neutral' without translation
common_failure_modes.detection: Scan user-facing rationale for any baseball-specific vocabulary not defined inline
common_failure_modes.fix: Translate every technical term on first use. Example: 'his expected stats (what his contact quality predicts) are lower than his actual stats (what he has produced), suggesting some luck'.

common_failure_modes.symptom: positional_need_fit < 30 and FAAB tight, but formula still recommends $3-5
common_failure_modes.detection: Check whether guardrail 4 should have fired; compute 0.15 x FAAB_original and compare to FAAB_remaining
common_failure_modes.fix: Apply guardrail 4. Default to $0 and flag zero_bid_preservation. Free claims are cost-free insurance.

common_failure_modes.symptom: Bid placed but no append-entry in tracker/faab-log.md showing winning_bid and industry_consensus
common_failure_modes.detection: Compare dates of decisions-log.md entries to faab-log.md entries; every bid should produce both
common_failure_modes.fix: After waiver period closes, append the faab-log row with winning_bid, winner, and industry_consensus_at_time. The 3-week outcome field can be filled later.

common_failure_modes.symptom: advocate and critic bids differ by >30% but output shows only synthesis bid with no flag
common_failure_modes.detection: Compare advocate_bid and critic_bid if both captured; if |diff| / max > 0.30, variant_divergence_applied must be flagged
common_failure_modes.fix: Apply guardrail 3. Set rec_bid to the critic's (lower) number and flag in output.
